metadata.title: Application for Accreditation of Foreign Manufacturers
metadata.note_on_filename: FILE MISLABELED — filename suggests ICH E5 ethnic factors but actual content is PMDA procedural guide for accreditation of foreign drug and quasi-drug manufacturers under Japan's Pharmaceutical Affairs Law (PAL)
metadata.issuing_authority: Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (PMDA), Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW)
metadata.deduplication_note: No prior coverage of foreign manufacturer accreditation rules found in output-v2/, output-v3/, output-v4/, or output-v5/. Extracted fresh.

rules.rule_text: A foreign manufacturer intending to manufacture drugs or quasi-drugs in foreign countries and export them to Japan must be accredited by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as an 'Accredited Foreign Manufacturer' under Article 13-3 of the Pharmaceutical Affairs Law (PAL), equivalent to the licensing requirement for domestic Japanese manufacturers.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — What is Accreditation of Foreign Manufacturers?
rules.verbatim_quote: A foreign manufacturer (a person/a company) intending to manufacture drugs, or quasi-drugs in foreign countries and export them to Japan, is required to be accredited by the Minister of Health, Labour, and Welfare as an 'Accredited Foreign Manufacturer', specified in Article 13-3 of PAL, in the same way that a Japanese manufacturer is licensed.
rules.exceptions: A foreign manufacturer whose marketing approval holder has an effective importing license granted under the old PAL as of April 1, 2005, is deemed to be temporarily accredited ('Deemed Accredited Foreign Manufacturer') until the end of its effective period.

rules.rule_text: A foreign manufacturer intending to manufacture only drug substances (APIs) to be exported to Japan must also obtain accreditation as an 'Accredited Foreign Manufacturer'. The accreditation is granted for each manufacturing establishment according to the category specified by the Enforcement Regulations.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — What is Accreditation of Foreign Manufacturers?
rules.verbatim_quote: a foreign manufacturer intending to manufacture only drug substances to be exported to Japan also need to obtain accreditation as an 'Accredited Foreign Manufacturer'. The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare has an authority to grant accreditation to a foreign manufacturer, while PMDA examines buildings and facilities of the manufacturing establishment for accreditation. The accreditation is granted for each manufacturing establishment according to the category specified by the Enforcement Regulations.

rules.rule_text: Before applying for accreditation, a Japanese marketing approval holder acting as an 'Applicant' must first submit a 'Business Number Registration Form' to obtain a 'Business Number', reporting information on the applicant's business and manufacturing establishments.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — What is Accreditation of Foreign Manufacturers?
rules.verbatim_quote: Before applying for accreditation, a Japanese marketing approval holder for an 'Applicant' needs to submit 'Business Number Registration Form', reporting information on the applicant's business and manufacturing establishments, in order to obtain 'Business Number'.

rules.rule_text: An applicant for accreditation must submit two forms in duplicate: (1) 'Application for Accreditation' (Form No. 18 in the PAL Enforcement Regulations) addressed to the Minister, and (2) 'Application for Accreditation Examination' (Form No. 16-(2) in the Regulations) to the Chief Executive of PMDA. Both must be submitted to Administration Division II, Office of Review Administration of PMDA.
rules.source_section: Section 2(1) — Application for Accreditation of Foreign Manufacturers
rules.verbatim_quote: An 'Applicant' is required to submit 'Application for Accreditation' (Form No. 18 in the PAL Enforcement Regulations) that is addressed to the Minister in duplicate, and 'Application for Accreditation Examination' (Form No.16-(2) in the Regulations) to Chief Executive of PMDA. Both applications need to be submitted to Administration Division II, Office of Review Administration of PMDA.
rules.exceptions: A Japanese marketing approval holder who markets drugs manufactured by a foreign manufacturer can make an accreditation application on the manufacturer's behalf; however, the 'Name of Applicant' field must contain the foreign manufacturer's name.

rules.rule_text: An applicant is responsible to renew their accreditation every 5 years. Failure to renew within the 5-year effective period causes the accreditation to become null and void.
rules.source_section: Section 2(1) and Section 2(4) — Application for Renewal of Accreditation
rules.verbatim_quote: an 'Applicant' is to be responsible to renew their accreditation every 5 years... Unless an 'Accredited Foreign Manufacturer' renews their accreditation, using a form of 'Application for Renewal of Accreditation' (Form No. 20 in the PAL Enforcement Regulations), within its 5-year effective period, their accreditation becomes null and void.

rules.rule_text: PMDA principally requires only a document audit fee for accreditation of foreign manufacturers and does not, in principle, conduct on-site inspections for the purpose of examining buildings and facilities of a foreign manufacturing establishment for accreditation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Examination Fees for the accreditation differ between on-site and document examinations. However, PMDA requires only document audit fee to be paid to our bank account because, in principle, we do not conduct on-site inspection only for the purpose of examining buildings and facilities of a foreign manufacturing establishment to be accredited.
rules.exceptions: On-site examination is possible in exceptional cases; fees differ between on-site and document examinations.

rules.rule_text: When an applicant intends to apply for a new accreditation, they cannot apply for multiple categories in one accreditation application. They must submit an accreditation application for one category and additional applications for the other categories.
rules.source_section: Section 2(1) — Application for Accreditation of Foreign Manufacturers
rules.verbatim_quote: When an 'Applicant' intends to apply for a new accreditation, they cannot apply for multiple categories in one accreditation application. They need to submit an accreditation application for one category and, at the same time, submit additional applications for the other categories.

rules.rule_text: The accreditation application must include the following attached documents per Article 36, Paragraph 3 of the PAL Enforcement Regulations: (1) curriculum vitae of the person responsible for the manufacturing establishment; (2) list of products to be manufactured for export to Japan and documents on manufacturing process; (3) documents on buildings and facilities; (4) documents on radiopharmaceuticals facilities (when applicable); and (5) a currently valid copy of the license certificate from the country where the foreign manufacturer resides.
rules.source_section: Section 2(2) — Documents to Be Attached to Accreditation Application
rules.verbatim_quote: 'A curriculum vitae of the person who is responsible to the manufacturing establishment'... 'List of products' to be manufactured for exporting to Japan and 'Documents on manufacturing process'... 'A document on buildings and facilities of a manufacturing establishment'... 'When a system for marketing license, manufacturing license, marketing approval or marketing certification of drugs or an equivalent system is established in the country where the foreign manufacturer resides, a copy of the license certificate issued by governmental organizations etc. of the country under such system'. The license certificate should be currently valid.
rules.exceptions: For quasi-drug manufacturers whose marketing approval holder responsibly ensures reliability, the CV of the responsible person does not need to be attached, and certain address fields on the application may be omitted.

rules.rule_text: An applicant intending to manufacture drugs (excluding in vitro diagnostics) and quasi-drugs must submit documents equivalent to the application documents for a domestic manufacturing license, as specified in the Enforcement of Ministerial Ordinance for Manufacturing Control and Quality Control of Drugs regulations, plus informative documents such as a floor plan.
rules.source_section: Section 2(2)(3) — Documents on buildings and facilities
rules.verbatim_quote: An 'Applicant' intending to manufacture drugs (excluding in vitro diagnostics) and quasi-drugs needs to submit documents equivalent to the application documents for manufacturing license of drugs and quasi-drugs specified in 'Enforcement of Ministerial Ordinance etc. of Partial Revision of the Regulations for Manufacturing Control and Quality Control of Drugs and Regulations for Buildings and Facilities for Pharmacies, etc.' (PAB Notification No. 1332, dated October 9, 1980). In addition, they need to submit informative documents for examination of buildings and facilities of their manufacturing establishments such as a floor plan.
rules.exceptions: In vitro diagnostics are excluded from this requirement.

rules.rule_text: When an Accredited Foreign Manufacturer changes their accredited category or adds new categories, they must submit 'Application for Change/Addition' (Form No. 21 in the PAL Enforcement Regulations) along with a new 'Application for Accreditation Examination' (Form No. 16-(2)). Applying for change in category allows accreditation for new categories while cancelling previously accredited categories.
rules.source_section: Section 2(3) — Application for Change/Addition in Category of Accreditation
rules.verbatim_quote: When an 'Accredited Foreign Manufacturer' changes the category accredited to their manufacturing establishment or adds new categories to their accreditation, they must submit a form, 'Application for Change/Addition' (Form No. 21 specified in the PAL Enforcement Regulations), in order to newly obtain the Minister's accreditation for the change or addition... applying for change in category of accreditation allows the manufacturer to be accredited to new categories while cancelling their previously accredited categories.

rules.rule_text: A Deemed Accredited Foreign Manufacturer seeking its first renewal under the revised PAL must attach a copy of the certificate for their importing license (granted under the old PAL) to their Japanese importer (marketing approval holder under the new PAL), in addition to following the standard new-accreditation procedure.
rules.source_section: Section 2(4) — Application for Renewal of Accreditation of Foreign Manufacturers
rules.verbatim_quote: When a 'Deemed Accredited Foreign Manufacturer' applies for the first renewal of their deemed accreditation after revision of the PAL, they need to attach a copy of the certificate for their importing license, which was granted under the old PAL to their Japanese importer called marketing approval holder under the new PAL, in addition to take the same procedure as to obtain a new accreditation.
rules.exceptions: Applies only to Deemed Accredited Foreign Manufacturers during their first renewal under the revised PAL.

rules.rule_text: When an Accredited or Deemed Accredited Foreign Manufacturer makes changes to specified matters, they must notify the Minister within 30 days by submitting a notification (Form No. 6) to PMDA. Reportable changes include: name/address of the responsible person, names of executives, name of the establishment, major building/facility changes, and category/accreditation number changes.
rules.source_section: Section 3(1) — Notification on Change (Article 100 of the PAL Enforcement Regulations)
rules.verbatim_quote: When an '(Deemed) Accredited Foreign Manufacturer' makes changes in the following matters, they must notify the fact to the Minister within 30 days by submitting a notification (Form No. 6) to PMDA.

rules.rule_text: For quasi-drug manufacturers, the applicant must indicate the GMP status of their intended products by showing either 'Quasi-drug Subject to GMP' or 'Quasi-drug Not Subject to GMP' in the remarks column of the application form.
rules.source_section: Section 2(2) — Points to Consider (PTC) with Respect to Accreditation of Foreign Manufacturers of Quasi-drugs
rules.verbatim_quote: the 'Applicant' needs to indicate the status of their intended products by showing either 'Quasi-drug Subject to GMP' or 'Quasi-drug Not Subject to GMP' in the remarks column.

rules.rule_text: In principle, document examination (rather than on-site inspection) is the required examination method for accreditation of foreign manufacturers. General application inquiries are handled by Administration Division II, Office of Review Administration, while building/facility examinations are handled by Division of Inspection for Drugs, Office of Manufacturing Quality for drugs.
rules.source_section: Section 3(2) — Advising Division/Office; Examinations in Accreditation flowchart (page 7)
rules.verbatim_quote: In principle, document examination is required for accreditation
rules.exceptions: On-site examination may occur in exceptional cases.
